title: 6 Using the Account Summary Function
body_text:
6	Using the Account Summary Function
6.1	Overview of the Account Summary Page
The Account Summary page on CAS Online displays the balance of each settlement account. This information is refreshed every 20-30 minutes. The way in which accounts are displayed on the Account Summary page depends on whether the financial institution centralizes settlement with Central 1.
If the financial institution has a centralized settlement account (i.e., transactions from multiple branches settle to one account), then only that account appears on the Account Summary page. Ontario credit unions are typically set up on CAS Online in this manner. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Account Summary Page â One Centralized Settlement Account
If the financial institution has multiple settlement accounts (i.e., transactions settle to different accounts by branch), multiple accounts appear on the Account Summary page. BC credit unions are typically set up on CAS Online in this manner. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Account Summary Page â Multiple Settlement Accounts
Regardless of whether the financial institution centralizes settlement, Canadian and US dollar transactions are posted to separate sub-accounts. If the financial institution maintains a Central 1 account in Euros or Pound Sterling, they are also displayed separately.
6.2	Accessing the Account Summary Page
The Account Summary page is the first page displayed upon login to CAS Online. See Figures 1 and 2 above.
Alternatively, select the
Accounts
tab at the top of the page, then select
Accounts Summary
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Accessing the Account Summary Page
6.3	Account Summary Interactive Features
Use the interactive features on the Account Summary page to better display account information. These features are most useful for financial institutions with multiple settlement accounts. See Figure 4.
â
Show # of entries
.
Use the drop-down menu to select the number of entries displayed per Account Summary page. For example, if the user selects 25, that is the maximum number of entries (i.e., sub-accounts by currency) displayed on the page regardless of whether they are spread across multiple accounts (Transit Numbers).
â¡
Search
.
Enter text in the Search box to search by account name or ID, sub-account name, or balance.
To request a consolidated balance, enter any of the following:
â
C:CAD
for Canadian dollar balances
â
C:USD
for US dollar balances
â
C:GBP
for Pound Sterling balances
â
C:EUR
for Euro balances
The consolidated balance is displayed above the search box. See Figure 5.
â¢
Sorting capability
.
Select
Account Name
or
Balance
to re-sort the account information.
â£
Interactive page view
. To navigate to additional Account Summary pages, select
a page number
,
Previous
, or
Next
in the bottom right corner.
Figure 4: Interactive Features on the Account Summary Page
Figure 5: Consolidated Account Balance
6.4	Viewing Recent Transactions
Recent transactions (those posted today) can be accessed via the Account Summary page. To view previous days transactions, use the Account Activity function as described in Chapter 7, Using the Account Activity Function.
To view recent transactions from the Account Summary page:
Select the applicable sub-account by currency on the Account Summary page. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Select Sub-Account on Account Summary Page
View the account transactions. See Figure 7 and Figure 8.
The following information is displayed:
â
Transaction date
â
A description of the transaction that includes the 2-character transaction code, a transaction description, and reference number details. See Transaction and Charge Codes, Form 3667, for a list of transaction codes and descriptions. For financial institutions with one settlement account (typically Ontario credit unions), the description also includes the source account details. The source account is the Transit Number associated with the transaction and is identified in the line âSrcAcc#nnnnnnnnâ. The source account is only displayed if it differs from the Transit Number displayed in the Member Number field.
â
Transaction amount
â
Account balance
Tip
:	Select a column header to re-sort the data.
Note:	A negative symbol (-) specifies a debit transaction.
Figure 7: Recent Transactions â One Settlement Account (Source Account Description)
Figure 8: Recent Transactions â Multiple Settlement Accounts (No Source Account Description)
END
